title: Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread

seo.title: Garlic Naan Recipe: Easy Homemade Indian Bread
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of homemade Garlic Naan with our easy recipe! Get soft, fluffy, and flavorful Indian bread, perfect for any meal. No tandoor needed!
lowCompetitionKeywords: Tandoor style Garlic Naan at home
h2Headings: Ingredients You'll Need for Garlic Naan
Pro Tips for the Best Homemade Naan
seo.imageAlt: Freshly baked homemade garlic naan bread on a wooden board, garnished with cilantro.
seo.imageCaption: Learn how to make the most delicious, fluffy garlic naan at home with our simple recipe!

description: Experience the joy of baking authentic Indian Garlic Naan right in your own kitchen. This recipe yields soft, chewy bread infused with fragrant garlic, perfect for scooping up your favorite curries or enjoying on its own. Impress your family and friends with this classic Indian flatbread.

instructions.title: Activate the Yeast
instructions.text: In a small bowl, combine the warm water, sugar, and active dry yeast. Stir gently and let it sit for about 5-10 minutes until the mixture becomes frothy and bubbly. This indicates that your yeast is active and ready to make your naan fluffy.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: In a large mixing bowl, combine the all-purpose flour, salt, yogurt, vegetable oil, and the activated yeast mixture. Mix everything together with a spoon or your hands until a shaggy dough forms. If the dough is too dry, add a tiny bit more warm water, one teaspoon at a time.

instructions.title: Knead the Dough
instructions.text: Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 7-10 minutes until it becomes smooth, elastic, and no longer sticky. Form the dough into a ball, lightly grease the bowl with a little oil, and place the dough back in. Cover the bowl with a clean kitchen towel or plastic wrap.

instructions.title: First Rise
instructions.text: Allow the dough to rise in a warm place for at least 1-1.5 hours, or until it has doubled in size. This rising time is crucial for developing the naan's characteristic soft and airy texture. You can place it in a slightly warm oven that is turned off.

instructions.title: Prepare Garlic Topping and Divide Dough
instructions.text: While the dough is rising, mix the minced garlic with the melted ghee or butter. Once the dough has risen, gently punch it down to release the air. Divide the dough into 6-8 equal portions and roll each portion into a smooth ball.

instructions.title: Roll and Cook Naan
instructions.text: Heat a cast-iron skillet or a heavy non-stick pan over high heat until it is very hot. On a lightly floured surface, roll out one dough ball into an oval or teardrop shape, about 6-8 inches long and 1/4 inch thick. Brush one side of the rolled naan with the garlic butter mixture and sprinkle with a little chopped cilantro.

instructions.title: Cook the Naan
instructions.text: Place the naan, buttered side down, onto the hot skillet. Cook for 1-2 minutes until bubbles appear on the surface and the bottom is lightly golden. Flip the naan and cook the other side for another 1-2 minutes until it puffs up and gets golden spots. Alternatively, you can finish by holding it directly over an open flame for a few seconds to get charred spots, just like a tandoor.

instructions.title: Serve Fresh
instructions.text: Remove the cooked naan from the skillet and brush immediately with more melted ghee or butter. Repeat the process with the remaining dough balls. Serve your freshly made Garlic Naan hot with your favorite Indian curries, dals, or as a side with any meal.

tips: For best results, ensure your water is at the correct temperature for activating yeast; too hot will kill it, too cold won't activate it.
If you dont have plain yogurt, you can use buttermilk or milk mixed with a teaspoon of lemon juice or vinegar as a substitute for a similar tenderizing effect.
Serve naan immediately as it is best fresh and warm. If you have leftovers, lightly warm them in a dry skillet or oven before serving.
article: Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread is a beloved Indian Baking dish enjoyed across Indian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
